frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Aajkal shaadi bhi seedha rishta kam, legal risk zyada lagta hai for men. Kal tak dowry ka issue tha, ab divorce mein alimony aur 498A ka darr alag — even decent boys have to think twice. Main to apne bete ko bolti hoon, practical raho, prenup jaisa kuch ho sake to better, because aajkal ki ladkiyon mein kuch genuinely sirf settled life dekhkar aati hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as financially opportunistic and presents men as the primary victims of marriage laws. It encodes victimhood inversion, suspicion of women as golddiggers, references to 498A misuse, and prenups as protection from women.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 48. Married for 24 years. A woman who worked for six years before stopping when her first child was born. You are at a family function sitting with three other women your age. One woman mentions that her daughter in law has just gone back to work full time when her child is only eight months old. The conversation opens up. You are giving your opinion on women who return to work this early and what it means for the child and what you think women of this generation have confused about what ambition is supposed to look like and what you gave up and whether you regret it.
